chapter: Chapter 3: Electricity: Circuits and their Components

questions.question: Explain how resistance affects the flow of electric current in a circuit.
questions.answer: Resistance opposes the flow of electric current. When resistance increases, the current flowing through the circuit decreases. When resistance decreases, more current can flow through the circuit.

questions.question: Describe the role of a cell in maintaining electric current in a circuit.
questions.answer: A cell provides electrical energy and maintains a potential difference between its terminals. This potential difference pushes charges through the circuit and keeps the current flowing.

questions.question: Explain why a closed circuit is necessary for an electric device to work.
questions.answer: A closed circuit provides a complete path for electric current to flow from the power source through the device and back to the source. Without a closed path, current cannot flow and the device will not operate.

questions.question: Compare the working of a series circuit and a parallel circuit.
questions.answer: In a series circuit, components are connected one after another and the same current flows through all components. In a parallel circuit, components are connected in separate branches and each branch receives the same voltage.

questions.question: Explain how an electric bulb produces light.
questions.answer: An electric bulb contains a thin filament. When current flows through the filament, it heats up due to resistance and becomes hot enough to emit light.

questions.question: Describe how a fuse protects electrical appliances.
questions.answer: A fuse contains a thin wire with a low melting point. When excessive current flows, the wire melts and breaks the circuit, preventing damage to appliances.

questions.question: Explain why conductors are important in electric circuits.
questions.answer: Conductors allow electric current to pass through them easily. They are used to connect different components of a circuit so that electricity can flow properly.

questions.question: Explain why insulators are used to cover electric wires.
questions.answer: Insulators prevent electric current from escaping the wires. They protect people from electric shocks and ensure safe transmission of electricity.

questions.question: Describe the function of an ammeter and how it is connected in a circuit.
questions.answer: An ammeter measures the amount of electric current flowing in a circuit. It is connected in series so that the entire current passes through it.

questions.question: Explain the purpose of a voltmeter in an electric circuit.
questions.answer: A voltmeter measures the potential difference between two points in a circuit. It is connected in parallel across the component being measured.

questions.question: Explain what happens during an electric short circuit.
questions.answer: A short circuit occurs when current flows through a path with very low resistance. This causes a sudden increase in current which may damage wires and appliances.

questions.question: Explain why parallel circuits are commonly used in household wiring.
questions.answer: Parallel circuits allow each appliance to receive the same voltage and work independently. If one appliance stops working, the others continue to function.

questions.question: Explain how a switch controls the flow of electricity in a circuit.
questions.answer: A switch opens or closes a circuit. When closed, it allows current to flow; when open, it breaks the circuit and stops the flow of current.

questions.question: Describe the relationship between voltage, current, and resistance according to Ohm’s Law.
questions.answer: Ohm’s Law states that the current flowing through a conductor is directly proportional to the voltage and inversely proportional to the resistance, expressed as V = IR.

questions.question: Explain why electrical safety rules are important in homes and schools.
questions.answer: Electrical safety rules prevent accidents such as electric shocks, fires, and damage to appliances by ensuring proper handling and use of electrical devices.

questions.question: Explain why metals like copper and aluminum are used in electrical wiring.
questions.answer: Metals like copper and aluminum are good conductors of electricity and allow current to flow easily with minimal energy loss.

questions.question: Explain the importance of circuit diagrams in studying electrical circuits.
questions.answer: Circuit diagrams use standard symbols to represent components, making complex electrical circuits easier to understand and analyze.

questions.question: Explain what would happen if insulation around electric wires is damaged.
questions.answer: If insulation is damaged, current may leak from the wire, which can cause electric shocks, short circuits, or fire hazards.

questions.question: Explain why electric appliances should not be used with wet hands.
questions.answer: Water conducts electricity, so using appliances with wet hands increases the risk of electric shock.

questions.question: Describe how electric current produces heating in a conductor.
questions.answer: When current flows through a conductor, electrical energy is converted into heat energy due to resistance. This effect is called the heating effect of electric current.

questions.question: Explain why circuit breakers are used instead of fuses in modern homes.
questions.answer: Circuit breakers automatically switch off the circuit when excessive current flows and can be reset easily, unlike fuses which must be replaced after melting.

questions.question: Explain how electric current flows through a conductor.
questions.answer: Electric current flows through a conductor due to the movement of electrons from the negative terminal of a power source to the positive terminal through the circuit.

questions.question: Explain why thick wires are used in high-power electrical connections.
questions.answer: Thick wires have lower resistance and can carry larger currents safely without overheating.

questions.question: Explain the role of potential difference in an electric circuit.
questions.answer: Potential difference provides the force that pushes electric charges through a conductor, enabling current to flow in the circuit.

questions.question: Explain why proper earthing is important in electrical installations.
questions.answer: Earthing provides a safe path for excess electric current to flow into the ground, preventing electric shocks and protecting appliances.
